{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Caro-Kann Exchange Variation| |rd| |bd|qd|kd|bd| |rd|= |pd|pd| | |pd|pd|pd|pd|= | | |nd| | |nd| | |= | | | |pd| | | | |= | | | |pl| | | | |= | | |pl|bl| | | | |= |pl|pl| | | |pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl||nl|rl|= |parent=[[Chess/Caro-Kann Defence|Caro-Kann]] }} = Caro-Kann Exchange Variation = This move develops the knight and threatens to develop the light-squared bishop to g4, attacking the queen. White usually continues with [[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...c6/2. d4/2...d5/3. exd5/3...cxd5/4. Bd3/4...Nc6/5. c3/5...Nf6/6. Bf4|6. Bf4]], allowing 6...Bg4, where white plays 7. Qb3, putting pressure on black's b- and d-pawns. White may also play 6. h3, still not allowing the prefered development of the c8-bishop. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}. 1.e4 c6 2.d4 d5 3.exd5 cxd5 4. Bd3 Nc6 5.c3 Nf6
